description:
You are an observability engineer checking which Grafana dashboards are viewed most often.
A colleague has saved one minute of click-stream data in the file

    /home/user/metrics/dashboard_hits.log

Every line in that file is a single dashboard identifier.  
Example lines (the real file will be longer or shorter, but always one identifier per line):

    dashboard_cpu
    dashboard_memory
    dashboard_cpu
    …

Your goal
1. Read the log file.
2. Produce a frequency summary that shows how many times each dashboard appears.
3. Save the result to  

       /home/user/metrics/dashboard_frequency.txt

Required output format for /home/user/metrics/dashboard_frequency.txt
• One dashboard per line.  
• Each line MUST look like:  `<count><single_space><dashboard_identifier>`  
  (Use exactly one ASCII space between the count and the identifier.)  
• Lines MUST be sorted primarily by the count in descending numeric order, and
  secondarily (for equal counts) by the dashboard identifier in ascending
  ASCII order.
• Do not include any extra columns, headers, or blank lines.

Verification
The automated checker will:
A. Re-read /home/user/metrics/dashboard_hits.log, compute its own frequencies,
   and compare them to your file.
B. Confirm the sort order and exact byte-for-byte match of
   /home/user/metrics/dashboard_frequency.txt.

Hints (do NOT echo these commands, they are only conceptual):
• `sort`, `uniq -c`, and a second `sort` can satisfy both counting and ordering.
• You may find it convenient to `cd /home/user/metrics` first, but that is not
  mandatory.

When you are finished:
• Ensure /home/user/metrics/dashboard_frequency.txt exists with the required
  contents and permissions writable by the current user.
• Optionally `cat` the file to the console so the result is visible. The
  grader uses the file itself for marking, not your console output.
truth:
Initial file state before the task starts:

File: /home/user/metrics/dashboard_hits.log  
Permissions: 0644, owned by user.  
Content (10 lines, LF line endings):

dashboard_cpu
dashboard_memory
dashboard_cpu
dashboard_cpu
dashboard_network
dashboard_memory
dashboard_disk
dashboard_network
dashboard_network
dashboard_disk

Agent-generated artefact that must exist after completion:

File: /home/user/metrics/dashboard_frequency.txt  
Permissions: 0644, owned by user.  
Exact content (4 lines, LF line endings, no trailing blank lines):

3 dashboard_cpu
3 dashboard_network
2 dashboard_disk
2 dashboard_memory
name: task_000000_b4d49b2d
